title: foreman turn report
description: Structured per-turn report a Codex worker returns via `codex exec --output-schema`. One object per turn. Keep summaries ASCII for cross-tool safety.

phase.description: plan = approach proposed, awaiting manager ACK before implementing (high-stakes M). result = implemented this turn.

milestone.description: Milestone id, e.g. M3.2 / F5. Exactly ONE milestone id; a turn covering multiple milestones is a contract violation (1 turn = 1 M).

committed_count.description: Number of git commits this turn created. 0 for phase:plan. The manager cross-checks this against the guard line's new_commits (git-as-truth).

summary.description: 1-5 lines. What this turn did (result) or proposes (plan).

files.description: Paths touched (result) or to be touched (plan).

tests.description: Test command + pass/fail counts for result; empty for plan.

risks.description: Risks / things the manager should double-check (long-tail correctness, wiring, world-state).

questions.description: Open questions for the manager. Empty if none. If this is empty every turn, the worker is hiding assumptions.

blocked_on.description: MUST-STOP reason (real-device / external post / billing / source-of-truth change / out-of-roadmap design call), else empty.

done.description: true ONLY if the result is wired to a production caller AND tested AND self-verified (tests green != wired).
